Name: Iceland
Intro text: Iceland had this cold beauty. People living in the shadows of the mountains and glaciers, in a place that felt like it was the end of the world. Signs pointed to specific houses, everyone was miles apart, but at the same time, shockingly multi-cultural. Everyone I met spoke multiple languages. And the sights were otherworldly.

Name: Family
Intro text: Everyone that knows me knows how important family is to me, and it’s hard living so far away, so I always try to go home and visit as much as I can. Lots of changes over the past few years so always trying to not take any moments for granted. On another note I love these photos because they’re either look like a portal to a different era/place (even though they’re just a few weeks old) or a perfect microcosm of what time with my family feels like to me.

Name: Cinque Terre
Intro text: The inspiration for Luca, Cinque Terre was incredible—walking from town to town, I was struck by how friendly everyone was. The villages felt connected, like a tight-knit community, and the whole place had a quiet, effortless charm that made it feel special.

Name: People of Sicily
Alt text for cover image: People sitting at a fountain in Taormina
Intro text: Sicily was a beautiful place filled with beautiful people living in a beautiful way. A constant reminder to slow down and enjoy a walk, or a espresso, or a nice view, or your neighbors.

Name: Cartagena de Indias
Alt text for cover image: Woman in Cartagena Colombia with the Palaqueras.
Intro text: Cartagena de Indias will always have a piece of my heart. The place where I learned Spanish, learned what it means to make a good arepa, and what it means to live with alegría.
